perturbation.original_text: Nantz will be a full time Ashworth employee rather than an independent contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: Nantz will be an independent contractor rather than a full time Ashworth employee.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding Nantz's employment status. Originally, he was defined as a full-time employee. Now, he's defined as an independent contractor. This contradicts with the section where Nantz agrees to be nominated, elected to and serve on the Board of Directors of Company in the capacity of voting director. Since, this is mainly the role of the employee, not a contractor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BOARD OF DIRECTORS. Nantz agrees to be nominated, elected to and serve on the Board of Directors of Company in the capacity of voting director.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text stated Nantz was a full-time employee. The changed text now states Nantz is an independent contractor. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, where Nantz agrees to serve on the Board of Directors, a role more commonly associated with employees than independent contractors. The change introduces an inconsistency regarding Nantz's role and responsibilities, thereby creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall reimburse Nantz Communications for expenses reasonably incurred by Nantz or Nantz Communications in connection with the Services to the Company including, but not limited to, first-class air fare, hotel accommodations, local transportation and meals. Nantz Communications shall furnish the Company with an itemized statement from time to time, together with, whenever possible, actual bills, receipts, and other evidence of expenditure. Nantz Communications shall be reimbursed within thirty (30) days after receipt by the Company of such itemized statements and attachments.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall reimburse Nantz Communications for expenses reasonably incurred by Nantz or Nantz Communications in connection with the Services to the Company including, but not limited to, first-class air fare, hotel accommodations, local transportation and meals. Nantz Communications shall furnish the Company with an itemized statement from time to time, together with, whenever possible, actual bills, receipts, and other evidence of expenditure. Nantz Communications shall be reimbursed within ninety (90) days after receipt by the Company of such itemized statements and attachments.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the reimbursement period for expenses. Originally, Nantz Communications was to be reimbursed within 30 days. Now, it's 90 days. This contradicts the prompt payment expectation, potentially affecting Nantz Communications' cash flow and creating a point of conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement after being given notice thereof and, where applicable, a reasonable opportunity to cure the failure (which shall be 10 days in the event of a failure to timely make a payment pursuant hereto; 30 days otherwise).
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the reimbursement period from 30 to 90 days. The contradicted text specifies a 10-day cure period for failure to timely make a payment. Since the changed text significantly extends the payment timeframe, it directly contradicts the provision allowing only 10 days to cure a late payment, creating a strong in-text contradiction, as a payment within 90 days could still be considered a failure to make a timely payment under the original 30-day requirement and may exceed the 10-day cure period.

perturbation.original_text: The first Option to purchase 40,000 Shares upon payment of the aggregate Option Share Price (as defined below) for the number of Shares so purchased shall become exercisable on June 1, 1999, unless this Agreement is terminated as provided herein prior to such date, in which case this Option shall be canceled. Once exercisable, this Option may be exercised in full or in any number of partial exercises or in combination with the full or partial exercise of any other Option for a period terminating upon the earlier to occur of (A) the fifth anniversary of the initial exercisability date or (B) the date of termination of this Agreement, as provided herein, if this Agreement is terminated prior to May 30, 2001.
perturbation.changed_text: The first Option to purchase 40,000 Shares upon payment of the aggregate Option Share Price (as defined below) for the number of Shares so purchased shall become exercisable on June 1, 1999, unless this Agreement is terminated as provided herein prior to such date, in which case this Option shall be canceled. Once exercisable, this Option may be exercised in full or in any number of partial exercises or in combination with the full or partial exercise of any other Option for a period terminating upon the earlier to occur of (A) the second anniversary of the initial exercisability date or (B) the date of termination of this Agreement, as provided herein, if this Agreement is terminated prior to May 30, 2001.
perturbation.explanation: The modification alters the timeframe for exercising the first option, which previously extended to the fifth anniversary of the initial exercisability date, to now only the second anniversary. This creates contradiction, because, in subsequent sections, the second and third options still have the fifth anniversary as their exercisability date, creating in-text contradiction and potential disagreement about when the options expire, and on the other hand, creates in-text contradiction, because, the options will all become exercisable after June 1, 2001.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The second Option to purchase up to an additional 40,000 shares upon payment of the aggregate Option Share Price for the number of Shares so purchased shall become exercisable on June 1, 2000, unless this Agreement is terminated as provided herein prior to such date, in which case this Option shall be canceled. Once exercisable, this Option may be exercised in full or in any number of partial exercises or in combination with the full or partial exercise of any other Option for a period terminating upon the earlier to occur of (A) the fifth anniversary of the initial exercisability date or (B) the date of termination of this Agreement, as provided herein, if this Agreement is terminated prior to May 30, 2001;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the exercisability timeframe for the first option from five years to two years. The contradicted text explicitly states that the second option has a five-year exercisability period from its initial date. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the duration for exercising the options, as the first option now has a different duration than the second option, leading to potential disputes over the terms and enforceability of the options.
